name: t2iapi

metadata.name: t2iapi
metadata.summary: T2I API for device communication in test scenarios

documentation:
Contains generated python files created from protobuf files.
The protobuf files contain the specification of the serialization used to communicate information in test scenarios.

classes.name: t2iapi.combined.service_pb2_grpc.CombinedServiceServicer

methods.name: t2iapi.combined.service_pb2_grpc.CombinedServiceServicer.CreateContextStateWithAssociationAndSetOperatingMode
documentation:
Create a new or adapt an existing context state with the requested @ContextAssociation and
set the requested @OperatingMode for the referenced operation.
The context state @Handle which was created or adapted shall be returned.
The manipulated states shall be persistent until a next manipulation call or an SDC operation invocation.
If the device is not able to maintain the static states, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.combined.service_pb2_grpc.CombinedServiceServicer.SetComponentActivationAndSetOperatingMode
documentation:
Request to set the @ActivationState of the component or metric with the given handle to the requested
ComponentActivation value and to set the @OperatingMode of the operation with the given handle to the requested
OperatingMode value.
The manipulated states shall be persistent until a next manipulation call or an SDC operation invocation.
If the device is not able to maintain the static states, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.combined.service_pb2_grpc.CombinedServiceServicer.SetAlertActivationAndSetOperatingMode
documentation:
Request to set the @ActivationState of the alert with the given handle to the requested AlertActivation value and
to set the @OperatingMode of the operation with the given handle to the requested OperatingMode value.
The manipulated states shall be persistent until a next manipulation call or an SDC operation invocation.
If the device is not able to maintain the static states, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.combined.service_pb2_grpc.CombinedServiceServicer.SetModeOfOperationAndSetOperatingMode
documentation:
Request to set the non-exclusive mode of operation represented by an enum metric with the given handle to the
requested value and set the @OperatingMode of the operation with the given handle to the requested OperatingMode
value.
The manipulated states shall be persistent until a next manipulation call or an SDC operation invocation.
If the device is not able to maintain the static states, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.
Service to handle combined manipulations which result from at least two different manipulations.

classes.name: t2iapi.combined.service_pb2_grpc.CombinedService

classes.name: t2iapi.activation_state.service_pb2_grpc.ActivationStateServiceServicer

methods.name: t2iapi.activation_state.service_pb2_grpc.ActivationStateServiceServicer.SetAlertActivation
documentation:
Set the ActivationState of the Alert with the given handle to the given AlertActivation value.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.activation_state.service_pb2_grpc.ActivationStateServiceServicer.SetComponentActivation
documentation:
Set the ActivationState of the DeviceComponent or Metric
with the given handle to the given ComponentActivation value.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.activation_state.service_pb2_grpc.ActivationStateServiceServicer.ComponentActivationTransition
documentation:
Ensure that the requested transition of the value of the ActivationState of the DeviceComponent or Metric with the
given handle from the starting ActivationState value, given as "start_activation" in the request, to the final
ActivationState value, given as "end_activation" in the request, happens during the remote procedure call.

methods.name: t2iapi.activation_state.service_pb2_grpc.ActivationStateServiceServicer.SetAlertConditionAndAlertSignalActivationState
documentation:
Set the ActivationStates for the given alert condition handle and alert signal handle to the given
AlertActivation values.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call or an SDC operation invocation.
If the device is not able to maintain the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.activation_state.service_pb2_grpc.ActivationStateServiceServicer.SetSystemSignalActivation
documentation: Set a SystemSignalActivation for a alert system with the given handle.
Service to handle activation manipulations.

documentation: Service to handle activation manipulations.

methods.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricServiceStub.__init__
documentation:
Constructor.

Args:
    channel: A grpc.Channel.
Service to handle metric manipulations.

classes.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricServiceServicer

methods.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricServiceServicer.SetMetricQualityValidity
documentation: Set the MetricQuality/@Validity of the metric with the given handle to the given validity value.

methods.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricServiceServicer.SetActivationStateAndUserConfirmableValue
documentation:
For the metric with the provided handle do the following:
- set the @ActivationState to the provided value
- set the pm:MetricValue/@Value resp. @Samples and confirm the value via the device ui
Ensure that the @ActivationState remains set for as long as the confirmable value is set.

methods.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricServiceServicer.SetIncorrectValue
documentation:
Set an incorrect value for the metric with the provided handle. If an incorrect value for the metric is already
present, a new incorrect value shall be set. The "incorrect value" refers to BICEPS
pm:AbstractMetricValue/MetricQuality/@Validity. A measured value that is incorrect from the perspective of
the measuring device.

methods.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricServiceServicer.CalibrateMetric
documentation: Start a new calibration for the metric with the provided handle and return the calibration values.

methods.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricServiceServicer.SetMetricValuesWithQualityMode
documentation:
Provide metric values with a specific MetricQuality/@Mode for the metric with the given handle.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricServiceServicer.SetMetricValuesInRange
documentation:
Set new metric value(s) (resulting in new pm:MetricValue/@Value resp. pm:MetricValue/@Samples) within a given range.
The range is defined by lower and upper limit values which shall not be violated.
If no limit values are requested (empty strings), a random but valid metric @Value or @Samples shall be set.
If a limit is requested, it shall not be violated by the new metric @Value or @Samples.
Even if a metric @Value or @Samples is already present, a new metric @Value or @Samples shall be provided.

methods.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricServiceServicer.SetMetricStatus
documentation:
Set the provided measurement, calculation or setting status for the metric of the provided handle. This is not the
AbstractMetricState/@ActivationState, but the internal state of the device.
The resulting AbstractMetricState/@ActivationState shall be persistent until the next manipulation call.
If the device is not able to maintain the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricServiceServicer.IsComputerControlled
documentation:
Requests if the given metric handle is a computer-controlled metric.

A computer-controlled metric is a metric with @MetricCategory ∈ { Set, Preset } which is automatically
adapted by the device under test, e.g. for an FiO2 closed-loop within a device.

methods.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricServiceServicer.ProvideMetricValueOrSamples
documentation:
Provide pm:MetricValue/@Value or pm:MetricValue/@Samples for the metric with the requested handle.

The goal of this rpc is to have values present continuously, the values themselves may change over time.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricServiceServicer.TransitionProvideValueToExpressNoValueAvailable
documentation:
For the metric with the provided handle perform the following transition:
- provide pm:MetricValue/@Value resp. @Samples and then
- express for the metric that no value is available.

methods.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricServiceServicer.ConveyMetricDemoValues
documentation:
Convey demo values for the metric with the given handle.
This means that the metric value is arbitrary and for demonstration purposes only.
Service to handle metric manipulations.

classes.name: t2iapi.metric.service_pb2_grpc.MetricService
documentation: Service to handle metric manipulations.
This class is part of an EXPERIMENTAL API.

classes.name: t2iapi.operation.service_pb2_grpc.OperationServiceServicer

methods.name: t2iapi.operation.service_pb2_grpc.OperationServiceServicer.SetOperatingMode
documentation:
This manipulation shall change the devices state, which results in setting the requested OperatingMode for the
given operation.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.operation.service_pb2_grpc.OperationServiceServicer.SetInvocationEffectiveTimeoutLessThanOrEqualToThreshold
documentation:
Set the @InvocationEffectiveTimeout of the pm:AbstractOperationDescriptor with the given handle to a value that is
less than or equal to the requested threshold.
Service to handle operation manipulations.

classes.name: t2iapi.alert.service_pb2_grpc.AlertServiceServicer

methods.name: t2iapi.alert.service_pb2_grpc.AlertServiceServicer.SetAlertConditionPresence
documentation:
The AlertConditionState/@Presence shall be transitioned to the requested value for the AlertCondition with the
given handle. Even if the @Presence already has the requested value.

methods.name: t2iapi.alert.service_pb2_grpc.AlertServiceServicer.AlertConditionEscalation
documentation: Start/Stop an Escalation/Deescalation of the AlertConditionPriority for a given alert condition handle.

methods.name: t2iapi.alert.service_pb2_grpc.AlertServiceServicer.SetAlarmSignalInactivationState
documentation:
Change the state of the alarm signal inactivation state represented by the AlertCondition with the given
handle as requested.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

Inactivation States can be for example:
- Global Audio Pause, could have an condition with the text "Audio Pause"
- "Pressures Off"
- Cardiac By-Pass Mode

methods.name: t2iapi.alert.service_pb2_grpc.AlertServiceServicer.SetAlertSystemNotFunctional
documentation:
Set the AlertSystem with the given handle to not functional.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.alert.service_pb2_grpc.AlertServiceServicer.InitiateAlarmOff
documentation: Initiate alarm off for the AlertCondition with the given handle.

methods.name: t2iapi.alert.service_pb2_grpc.AlertServiceServicer.SetAsActivationStateOnAndChangeAcPresenceFalse
documentation:
For the AlertSignal with the provided handle ensure that the following sequence appears
- State 1
- pm:AlertSignalState/@Presence must be "On" or "Ack"
- pm:AlertSignalState/@ActivationState must be "On"
- the associated pm:AlertConditionState/@Presence must be "true"
- State 2
- pm:AlertSignalState/@ActivationState must be "On"
- the associated pm:AlertConditionState/@Presence must be "false"

methods.name: t2iapi.alert.service_pb2_grpc.AlertServiceServicer.SetSomeAlertSignalPresence
documentation:
Set the pm:AlertSignalState/@Presence for a subset of requested alert signals to the requested
@Presence value(s).
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.
Service to handle alert manipulations.

classes.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.SetLocationDetail
documentation:
Use the given LocationDetail in a currently associated LocationContextState or associate a new LocationContextState
with the LocationDetail.
The goal of this rpc is to have an associated LocationContextState, that contains the given LocationDetail.
This can for example be achieved by changing the location of the device in its settings UI.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.RemoveAllContextStateValidators
documentation:
Remove all Validators elements for the AbstractContextState with the provided context state handle.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.SetContextStateAssociation
documentation:
Set the ContextAssociation of the target ContextState to the given value.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.CreateContextStateWithAssociation
documentation:
Create or adapt a ContextState instance with the given ContextAssociation value for the given descriptor handle.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.CreateContextStateWithAssociationAndValidators
documentation:
Create or adapt a pm:AbstractContextState instance with the given @ContextAssociation value for the given descriptor handle
and provide at least the number of pm:Validator elements for this ContextState.
If the number of pm:Validator elements is zero, zero pm:Validator elements shall be provided.

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.CreateContextStateWithAssocIdentificationAndValidator
documentation:
Create or adapt a ContextState instance with the given ContextAssociation value for the given descriptor handle
and provide at least one Identification and Validator for this ContextState.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.TransitionFromCreateStateWithIdentificationToRemoveIdentification
documentation:
For the Context with the provided descriptor handle perform the following transition:
- associate a pm:AbstractContextState instance with at least one unique pm:Identification element
for the Context with the given descriptor handle
- remove at least one pm:Identification element for the previously associated pm:AbstractContextState.

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.TransitionFromCreateStateWithIdentificationToChangeIdentification
documentation:
For the Context with the provided descriptor handle perform the following transition:
- associate a pm:AbstractContextState instance with at least one unique pm:Identification element
for the Context with the given descriptor handle
- change at least one already present pm:Identification element for the previously associated pm:AbstractContextState.

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.CreateContextStateWithAssocAndSpecificValidator
documentation:
Create or adapt a ContextState instance with the given ContextAssociation value for the given descriptor handle,
confirm by requested validator type.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.CreateNeonatalPatientWithAssociationAndMothersIdentification
documentation:
Create or adapt neonatal patient with a given type of association and
including the mothers identification.

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.AssociatePatient
documentation:
Create or adapt a PatientContextState with CoreData/PatientType having the requested patient type.
The PatientContextState/@ContextAssociation shall be "Associated".

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.EnsembleContextIndicateMembership
documentation: Indicate membership in an SDC PARTICIPANT ENSEMBLE using an ensemble context, indicated by its descriptor handle.

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.EnsembleContextIndicateMembershipWithIdentification
documentation:
Indicate membership in an SDC PARTICIPANT ENSEMBLE using ensemble context state(s) that have the provided descriptor
handle and return a list of pm:Identification elements which identify the ensemble context state(s).
If already one or more pm:EnsembleContextState(s) indicate the membership, it is not needed to create a new
pm:EnsembleContextState(s). In this case it is sufficient to only return the BasicResponse and the pm:Identification
elements.
The state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain the static state,
it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.GetSupportedContextTypes
documentation: Returns all types of contexts which are supported by the DUT.

methods.name: t2iapi.context.service_pb2_grpc.ContextServiceServicer.AssociatePatientWithAutoGeneratedPatientId
documentation: Create or adapt a pm:PatientContextState having an associated patient with an auto generated patient id.

methods.name: t2iapi.device.service_pb2_grpc.DeviceServiceServicer.SetDeviceOperatingMode
documentation:
Switch the device into a specific operating mode.
This manipulation shall result in a Mds having a specific MdsOperatingMode.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.device.service_pb2_grpc.DeviceServiceServicer.SendHello
documentation: Announce the presence of the device in the network via a WS-Discovery Hello message.

methods.name: t2iapi.device.service_pb2_grpc.DeviceServiceServicer.SetClockDevice
documentation:
Adjust the clock of the device as if it were set manually by a user.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.device.service_pb2_grpc.DeviceServiceServicer.SetLanguage
documentation:
Set the device's (displayed) language to the given language.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call. If the device is not able to maintain
the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.

methods.name: t2iapi.device.service_pb2_grpc.DeviceServiceServicer.GetRemovableDescriptorsOfClass
documentation:
Get all descriptor handles which can be removed and reinserted into the MDIB of the device
and whose descriptors are of the given class.
Includes handles which have been removed already and can be reinserted.
Handles must stay the same once reinserted into the MDIB.

Calling GetRemovableDescriptorsOfClass with descriptor_class = DESCRIPTOR_CLASS_ABSTRACT
returns the unfiltered handles while other values for descriptor_class
return only handles whose descriptors are of the given class.

The unfiltered handles shall be representative of the devices capabilities
to remove descriptors (at least one of every possible kind).

methods.name: t2iapi.device.service_pb2_grpc.DeviceServiceServicer.RemoveDescriptor
documentation: Remove a descriptor from the device MDIB.

methods.name: t2iapi.device.service_pb2_grpc.DeviceServiceServicer.InsertDescriptor
documentation: Insert a descriptor into the device MDIB.

methods.name: t2iapi.device.service_pb2_grpc.DeviceServiceServicer.InsertMdsDescriptor
documentation: Insert an mds descriptor into the device MDIB.

methods.name: t2iapi.device.service_pb2_grpc.DeviceServiceServicer.RemoveMdsDescriptor
documentation: Remove an mds descriptor from the device MDIB.

methods.name: t2iapi.device.service_pb2_grpc.DeviceServiceServicer.TriggerDescriptorUpdate
documentation:
Trigger a DescriptionManipulationReport that fulfills the following:

For each given handle there exists an "Upt"-ReportPart that contains an updated descriptor, whose @DescriptorHandle
is equal to the given handle, and that is contained in the DescriptionModificationReport.

---

Note that when no handle is given, every DescriptionModificationReport fulfills the request.

methods.name: t2iapi.device.service_pb2_grpc.DeviceServiceServicer.TriggerAnyDescriptorUpdate
documentation:
Trigger a descriptor update.
The descriptor to update has to be chosen by the device. It is expected that this manipulation succeeds
as long as there are descriptors than can be updated.
This manipulation shall result in a msg:DescriptionModificationReport message containing a report part with
msg:ReportPart/@ModificationType = "Upt".

methods.name: t2iapi.device.service_pb2_grpc.DeviceServiceServicer.TriggerReport
documentation:
Trigger a report message of the provided type.
This manipulation of the device shall result in an msg:AbstractReport message of the provided type.

methods.name: t2iapi.device.service_pb2_grpc.DeviceServiceServicer.SetBatteryUsage
documentation:
Use or not use the device's battery as the electrical power source.
The manipulated state shall be persistent until a next manipulation call or until power failure. If the device is
not able to maintain the static state, it shall return RESULT_NOT_SUPPORTED.
